title: Claro in Panama
text: Have a house in the mountains around Boquete Panama where I always had to go up the steep bank behind the house to get any cell reception. Put this antenna up there and ran 150 ft. of RG6 coax to the booster in the house and now have ability to use phone and internet on phone in the house for first time. title: Very good for its intended purpose
text: The biggest issue with signal boosters is they have to use the same frequency coming in as going out. This creates the potential for a feedback look that shuts the device down.  Wilson not being stupid and having a lot of experience has gotten around this with a VERY short reception area for the cell phone.  For optimum results the phone HAS to be in the cradle and the system works quite well as long as there is a tower for your carrier within reach.  Having an understanding of how cell phones work and the what it takes to boost a signal is quite helpful in obtaining optimum results.
